frameworks.name: global theories of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: broad-function accounts the chapter argues overstate consciousness's utility

frameworks.name: metacognition
frameworks.one_line: candidate specific function the author thinks consciousness genuinely contributes to

frameworks.name: subliminal priming paradigm
frameworks.one_line: standard nonconscious-processing method whose small effects motivate new tools

frameworks.name: evolutionary functionalism of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: framing that consciousness arose recently and may be byproduct or specific adaptation

core_question: What, if any, specific cognitive function is consciousness genuinely useful for?
key_assumptions: consciousness emerged at some point in evolutionary history
simple organisms (e.g., bacteria) are not conscious
subliminal priming yields only small, often unreplicable nonconscious effects
stronger nonconscious signals are needed to test consciousness's added value
interpretations: current evidence suggests consciousness may be less broadly useful than global theories claim
metacognition may be one of the few specific functions to which consciousness contributes
limitations_noted: subliminal-priming effects are small and hard to replicate
